summarization.a: Plan-view TEM micrograph of a TiN film deposited by PEALD at 350 °C, showing a nanocrystalline microstructure with visible grain contrast at the ~10 nm scale.
summarization.b: Plan-view TEM micrograph of a Ti–Si–N film deposited by PEALD at 350 °C, showing a much more homogeneous contrast with no clearly resolved grains at the ~10 nm scale.

a.question: What microstructure is visible in the plan-view TEM of the pure TiN PEALD film?
a.answer: Polycrystalline with clear grain contrast and lattice fringes.

a.question: How does silicon incorporation affect the microstructure of PEALD TiN films?
a.answer: Silicon addition (≈11 at.%) completely suppresses crystallization, transforming the film from polycrystalline (a) to fully amorphous (b).

b.question: What microstructure is observed in the plan-view TEM of the Ti–Si–N PEALD film?
b.answer: Fully amorphous – completely featureless, no grains or lattice fringes at 10 nm scale.

b.question: Is the amorphous microstructure of Ti–Si–N advantageous for diffusion barrier applications?
